name: E.W. SCRIPPS Co
business: E.W. Scripps Co operates a portfolio of local television stations and national news and entertainment networks, reaching audiences through over-the-air, cable, satellite, and streaming platforms. It generates revenue from advertising, distribution fees, and content agreements. The company also owns the Scripps National Spelling Bee and sells a television recording device called Tablo.
sellsTo: political candidates and political action groups

exposures.sentence: Leveraging broadcasters uniquely efficient network architecture and the ATSC 3.0 transmission standard, EdgeBeam Wireless, LLC will provide expansive, reliable and secure data delivery services.

We will continue to face cybersecurity and similar risks, which could result in the disclosure of confidential information, disruption of operations, damage to our brands and reputation, legal exposure and financial losses.
In 2025, Scripps launched Engine Room, an enterprise Artificial Intelligence ("AI") platform designed to support both organization-wide capability building and function-specific skill development.

catalysts.event: Formation of EdgeBeam Wireless joint venture
catalysts.sentence: In January 2025, we announced the formation of a joint venture with Gray Media, Nexstar Media Group, Inc. and Sinclair, Inc.

catalysts.event: Multi-year agreement with Las Vegas Aces
catalysts.sentence: On March 13, 2025, we announced a multi-year agreement with the Las Vegas Aces, which began in May 2025.
On April 10, 2025, we completed a series of previously announced refinancing transactions.

catalysts.event: Multi-year media rights agreement with Tampa Bay Lightning
catalysts.sentence: On May 14, 2025, we announced a multi-year media rights agreement which allows us to produce and distribute all preseason, regular season and first-round playoff Tampa Bay Lightning games that are not allocated exclusively to national broadcasts.

On June 13, 2025, we announced a new, multi-year agreement with the Women's National Basketball Association ("WNBA") to continue airing regular season Friday night matchups on ION as part of its WNBA Fright Night Spotlight series.

catalysts.event: Station swap agreement with Gray Media
catalysts.sentence: On July 7, 2025, we entered into agreements with Gray Media, Inc. ("Gray"), to swap television stations across five markets.

catalysts.event: Issuance of senior secured second lien notes
catalysts.sentence: On August 6, 2025, we issued $750 million of senior secured second lien notes (the "2030 Senior Notes"), which bear interest at a rate of 9.875% per annum and mature on August 15, 2030.
On September 3, 2025, we reached an agreement to sell WFTX, our local Fox-affiliated station in Fort Myers, Florida, for $40.0 million.
In October 2025, we reached agreement to sell WRTV, our local ABC- affiliated station in Indianapolis, Indiana, for $83.0 million.
In the fourth quarter of 2025, we committed to the sale of Court TV and closed on the sale of the network on February 9, 2026.

catalysts.event: Exercise of call options for INYO stations
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, we notified INYO of our exercise of all of the options.

In February 2026, we announced an enterprise-wide transformation plan that is designed to improve operating performance and unlock new value and targets annualized enterprise EBITDA growth of $125 million to $150 million by 2028.
